sanskrit:
अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः।
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः॥५॥
transliteration:
aśāstra-vihitaṃ ghoraṃ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ |
dambhāhaṅkāra-saṃyuktāḥ kāma-rāga-balānvitāḥ ||5||
literal_meaning:
Not-by-scripture enjoined / terrible / they-afflict / who / austerity / people //
with-hypocrisy-and-ego / by-lust-and-attachment-impelled //
contextual_meaning: Self-harm disguised as virtue: when rigid perfectionism, social-media virtue-signalling, or extremist fasting masquerade as ‘discipline’ while actually feeding pride and resentment.
psychological_state: Narcissistic ascetic: uses ‘discipline’ to dominate others and suppress inner worthlessness; body and conscience become battlegrounds for control.
trigger_scenario: Influencer posts 7-day water-fast for ‘climate justice’, shames followers for eating, secretly binges at night—followers copy, end up in ER.
anchor_text: True discipline humbly serves life, not ego.
